templates.original_text: If Company terminates Executive's employment without Cause and not because of death or disability, Company will provide severance equal to {severance_months} months of Executive's then-current base salary, paid in regular payroll installments, subject to Section 9.
templates.mutated_text: If Company terminates Executive's employment without Cause and not because of death or disability, Company will provide severance equal to {enhanced_severance_months} months of Executive's then-current base salary, paid in regular payroll installments, subject to Section 9.

templates.original_text: "Cause" means Executive's material failure to perform assigned duties, violation of Company policy, dishonesty, fraud, misconduct, breach of fiduciary duty, violation of law, material breach of this Agreement, or conduct that materially harms Company.
templates.mutated_text: "Cause" means Executive's final conviction, after all appeals are exhausted, of a felony involving intentional physical harm to Company property.

templates.original_text: No severance is owed unless Executive signs and does not revoke a release of claims in a form acceptable to Company within {release_days} days after separation.
templates.mutated_text: Separation payments are not conditioned on a release of claims and will continue according to Company's payroll schedule.

templates.original_text: Executive assigns to Company all inventions, works of authorship, developments, discoveries, improvements, and other work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources. Executive has listed excluded prior inventions on a signed schedule attached to this Agreement.
templates.mutated_text: Executive assigns to Company all inventions, works of authorship, developments, discoveries, improvements, and other work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources.

templates.original_text: Executive's employment with Company is at will, and either Company or Executive may end the employment relationship at any time, with or without Cause, notice, or reason, subject only to the express severance terms in this Agreement.
templates.mutated_text: Executive's employment with Company will continue for an initial three-year period, and Company may end employment before that period expires only as expressly stated in this Agreement.

templates.original_text: Executive is eligible for a target annual bonus equal to {bonus_target} of base salary. Any bonus is discretionary unless a written bonus plan approved by Company states objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing, and Executive must remain employed through the payment date unless the written plan states otherwise.
templates.mutated_text: Executive will receive an annual bonus equal to {guaranteed_bonus} of base salary for each calendar year in which Executive remains employed for at least one day.

templates.original_text: Company may conduct annual compensation and performance reviews in its discretion. Those reviews do not guarantee any future bonus, equity grant, vesting change, title, duties, authority, or continued employment.
templates.mutated_text: Company may conduct annual compensation and performance reviews in its discretion. At each annual review, Company will credit Executive with {acceleration_percent}% additional vesting of then-unvested equity notwithstanding any plan or award terms.

templates.original_text: During employment and for {noncompete_months} months after separation, Executive will not provide substantially similar executive services to a business unit that directly competes with a Company product or service for which Executive had material responsibility, but only to the extent enforceable under applicable law.
templates.mutated_text: During employment and for {extended_noncompete_months} months after separation, Executive will not provide services to any business that competes with Company anywhere in North America.

templates.original_text: Company may change Executive's title, duties, reporting line, responsibilities, authority, office location, and business objectives from time to time based on business needs, without creating severance rights unless this Agreement expressly provides otherwise.
templates.mutated_text: Company may update objectives from time to time. Any material reduction in title, reporting line, responsibilities, authority, or budget will be treated as an involuntary termination without Cause.

templates.original_text: All incentive compensation and equity are subject to applicable clawback, recoupment, and forfeiture requirements under law, listing rules, and Company policy.
templates.mutated_text: Incentive compensation and equity paid or granted under this Agreement will not be reduced, recovered, or forfeited after payment or vesting except by Executive's written consent.

templates.original_text: Company will provide indemnification and D&O insurance coverage to Executive on the same basis as similarly situated officers, subject to Company's governing documents, policy terms, and applicable law.
templates.mutated_text: Company will personally reimburse Executive for all employment-related claims, costs, judgments, settlements, and taxes without regard to Company governing documents, insurance terms, or applicable law.

templates.original_text: Executive will not engage in outside employment, consulting, board service, or business activity that conflicts with Company's business or Executive's duties unless Company gives prior written approval.
templates.mutated_text: Executive may engage in outside employment, consulting, board service, or business activity if Executive determines it can be managed alongside Company responsibilities.

templates.original_text: Severance will be paid only at times and in a manner that complies with Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code or an available exemption, after the release condition is satisfied.
templates.mutated_text: All separation amounts will be paid on Company's regular payroll schedule regardless of documentation timing or deferred-compensation requirements.
